title: Extract CPUSchedulingCanvas from OSModule.tsx into standalone component
description: Part of OSC-028 decomposition. Extract lines 2364-2487 (cpu-scheduling canvas branch) into src/components/modules/os/CPUSchedulingCanvas.tsx. Pass scheduling state as props. Component receives: schedAlgo, processes, schedResult, comparisonResults, + callbacks.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: src/components/modules/os/CPUSchedulingCanvas.tsx
All CPU scheduling UI (controls, process cards, Gantt, comparison panel) moved
Props interface clearly typed
No functional regression
OSModule.tsx shrinks by ~120 lines

acceptanceCriteria: New file: src/components/modules/os/PageReplacementCanvas.tsx
All page replacement UI moved
Belady's Anomaly panel included
No functional regression

title: Extract MemAllocCanvas from OSModule.tsx

title: Extract ThreadSyncCanvas panel from OSModule.tsx

All thread sync UI moved

title: Extract useOSModule hook into separate file
description: Part of OSC-028. Extract lines 1981-3929 (useOSModule hook + all state + all callbacks) into src/hooks/useOSModule.ts. The hook is 1950 lines with 38 useState calls. After canvas extraction, it becomes a pure state management file.
acceptanceCriteria: New file: src/hooks/useOSModule.ts
All state, callbacks, and canvas composition logic moved
OSModule.tsx becomes a thin wrapper (<100 lines)
No functional regression

title: Add ARIA labels and roles to OSSidebar + concept buttons
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-023. Sidebar at OSModule.tsx:1334-1368. Add: role='navigation' to sidebar container, aria-label='OS Concepts navigation', aria-current='page' to active concept button, aria-describedby linking to concept descriptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar has role='navigation' and aria-label
Active concept button has aria-current='page'
Each concept button has aria-describedby linking to its description text
Screen reader announces 'OS Concepts navigation, CPU Scheduling selected'

title: Add ARIA to all form controls — htmlFor/id pairing + labels
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-023. Every <label> in OSModule.tsx lacks htmlFor. Every <input> and <select> lacks id. Count: ~15 label elements, ~20 input elements, ~8 select elements. Add htmlFor/id pairs throughout. Replace outline-none with visible focus ring.
acceptanceCriteria: Every label has htmlFor matching an input/select id
Every input/select has a unique id
All outline-none replaced with focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary
Tab order flows: controls → canvas → properties

title: Add ARIA to SVG visualizations — role='img' + title + desc
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-023. GanttChart (line 170), ResourceAllocationGraph (line 926), ThreadSyncCanvas (line 1102) — all SVGs lack role='img', <title>, <desc>, and aria-label. Add text alternatives for each SVG that describe what the visualization shows.
acceptanceCriteria: GanttChart SVG: role='img', aria-label='Gantt chart showing process execution timeline'
RAG SVG: role='img', <title>Resource Allocation Graph</title>, <desc> describing nodes and edges
ThreadSync SVG: role='img', aria-label describing thread states
All SVG text elements have aria-hidden='true' (decorative) or meaningful accessible names

title: Add aria-live regions for simulation results and step changes
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-023. When user clicks Run, results appear silently. When stepping, the event description changes silently. Add aria-live='polite' to: result summary area, current step description, properties panel metrics, error messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Result summary (Gantt, PageFrameGrid, etc.) wrapper has aria-live='polite'
Current step description text has aria-live='polite'
Properties panel metrics have aria-live='polite'
Error/validation messages have aria-live='assertive'

title: Add unit tests for scheduling.ts — SJF, SRTF, Priority (preemptive + non-preemptive)
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-035. scheduling.ts has tests only for FCFS and RR (31 lines). SJF, SRTF (preemptive), Priority (preemptive + non-preemptive) have ZERO coverage. Add: SJF correctness (selects shortest job), SRTF preemption on new short arrival, Priority ordering, Priority preemption.
acceptanceCriteria: sjf() non-preemptive: correct selection of shortest burst
sjf(preemptive=true): preempts when shorter job arrives
priorityScheduling() non-preemptive: correct priority ordering
priorityScheduling(preemptive=true): preempts on higher priority arrival
Edge cases: all same burst, all same priority, single process

title: Add unit tests for page-replacement.ts — Clock algorithm + zero-frame guard + Optimal
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-035. Clock has ZERO coverage and a confirmed while(true) bug on frameCount=0. Optimal has no correctness test. Add: Clock basic correctness, Clock zero-frame guard, Optimal produces fewest faults vs FIFO/LRU on classic ref string.
acceptanceCriteria: clockPageReplacement: basic fault counting correctness
clockPageReplacement(refs, 0): does NOT infinite loop (tests guard)
optimalPageReplacement: produces ≤ faults than FIFO and LRU on same input
All 4 algorithms: hitRate + faultRate === 1.0
Edge: empty reference string → valid empty result

title: Add unit tests for thread-sync.ts — Mutex, Semaphore, RW Lock
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-035. All 3 sync primitives have ZERO test coverage. Add: Mutex produces acquire/release/blocked events. Semaphore respects permit count. RW Lock allows multiple readers but exclusive writer. Writer priority respected.
acceptanceCriteria: simulateMutex(4): produces acquire events for each thread, only one holds at a time
simulateSemaphore(4, 2): at most 2 threads holding simultaneously
simulateReaderWriterLock(3, 2): multiple readers allowed, writer is exclusive
RW Lock: writer priority — waiting writer blocks new readers
All: MAX_TICKS guard prevents infinite simulation

title: Add unit tests for memory.ts — TLB hit/miss/eviction + page fault handling
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-035. simulateVirtualMemory() has ZERO test coverage. Add: TLB hit on repeated access, TLB miss + PT hit on first access after TLB eviction, page fault on uncached page, LRU frame eviction order, TLB eviction when full.
acceptanceCriteria: Repeated access to same address → TLB hit
Access after TLB eviction → TLB miss, PT hit
Access to new page beyond physical frames → page fault + LRU eviction
TLB size respected — oldest entry evicted when full
Edge: empty access pattern → valid empty result
Edge: pageSize not power of 2 → throws error

title: Add unit tests for memory-alloc.ts — fragmentation calculation + strategy comparison
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-035. All allocation strategies + fragmentation formula untested. Add: First Fit selects first sufficient block, Best Fit selects tightest, Worst Fit selects largest. Fragmentation formula correct. Deallocation merges adjacent free blocks.
acceptanceCriteria: simulateFirstFit: allocates in first block that fits
simulateBestFit: allocates in tightest fitting block
simulateWorstFit: allocates in largest block
calcFragmentation: 1 - (largestFree/totalFree) × 100 verified
Deallocation: adjacent free blocks merged correctly
Failed allocation: produces step with FAILED description

title: Add WHY descriptions to scheduling.ts step events — FCFS + SJF
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-077. Rewrite step descriptions in scheduling.ts for FCFS (lines 135-178) and SJF (lines 224-370). OLD: 'P1 starts execution (burst=5)'. NEW: 'P1 starts execution (burst=5). FCFS selected P1 because it arrived first — this algorithm serves processes in arrival order without interruption.'
acceptanceCriteria: FCFS 'start' event: explains arrival-order selection rule
FCFS 'complete' event: shows wait time and explains Convoy Effect if applicable
SJF 'start' event: explains shortest-burst selection
SJF 'preempt' event (SRTF): explains why shorter job preempts
Idle events: explain why CPU sits idle (no processes ready)

title: Add WHY descriptions to scheduling.ts — Round Robin + Priority
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-077. Rewrite step descriptions for RR (lines 389-512) and Priority (lines 524-685). RR 'preempt': 'P1 preempted after quantum=2 — Round Robin guarantees fairness by limiting each process to the quantum before yielding to the next.' Priority 'start': 'P2 starts (priority=1). Priority scheduling selected P2 because lower number = higher priority.'
acceptanceCriteria: RR 'start': mentions quantum and time-sharing concept
RR 'preempt': explains fairness guarantee through quantum-based rotation
Priority 'start': explains lower-number-higher-priority convention
Priority 'preempt' (preemptive): explains why higher priority arrival interrupts
All descriptions a beginner could understand without external reference

title: Add WHY descriptions to page-replacement.ts — all 4 algorithms
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-077. Rewrite step descriptions for FIFO, LRU, Optimal, Clock. FIFO eviction: 'Page 2 replaces page 7 (FIFO evicts the OLDEST loaded page — page 7 was loaded at t=0, the earliest in the frame set).' LRU eviction: 'LRU evicts page 3 because it was accessed longest ago — temporal locality suggests recently used pages are likely needed again.'
acceptanceCriteria: FIFO: eviction explains circular pointer and oldest-loaded rule
LRU: eviction explains recency principle and temporal locality
Optimal: eviction explains future-knowledge and why it's a theoretical benchmark
Clock: eviction explains second-chance mechanism (ref bit check → clear → advance)
HIT events: explain why hit means no page fault needed

title: Add WHY descriptions to deadlock.ts — safe sequence logic + cycle detection
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-077. Rewrite step descriptions for detectDeadlock and bankersAlgorithm. Safe sequence: 'P0 can finish because its remaining need [0,1,0] fits within available resources [3,3,2] — meaning the OS can guarantee P0 completes and releases its allocation.' Deadlock: 'P1 is deadlocked — it holds R2 but needs R1, which is held by P0, who needs R2 (circular wait).'
acceptanceCriteria: Safe sequence step: explains WHY process can finish (need ≤ available)
Resource release step: explains freed resources increase available pool
Deadlock detected: names the circular wait chain explicitly
Banker's denial: explains which resource constraint prevents safe state

title: Add WHY descriptions to memory.ts — TLB hit/miss/fault explanations
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-077. Rewrite step descriptions for virtual memory translation. TLB HIT: 'TLB HIT: VA 16 → page 1 → frame 2 → PA 32. The TLB cached this mapping from a previous access — skipping the slow page table lookup (100x faster).' PAGE FAULT: 'PAGE FAULT: VA 48 → page 3 not in physical memory. The OS must load this page from disk — this takes ~1ms, during which the process is blocked.'
acceptanceCriteria: TLB HIT: explains why TLB is fast (cache of recent translations)
TLB MISS + PT HIT: explains TLB miss requires page table walk
PAGE FAULT: explains disk I/O required and process blocking
Frame eviction: explains LRU policy and which frame was oldest
Non-standard 'PT HIT' renamed to 'TLB Miss, Page Table Hit'

title: Add WHY descriptions to thread-sync.ts — ownership + blocking explanations
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-077. Rewrite step descriptions for Mutex, Semaphore, RW Lock. Mutex acquire: 'T0 acquires the mutex — T0 is now the OWNER and only T0 can release it (this ownership is what distinguishes a mutex from a semaphore).' Semaphore acquire: 'T1 acquires a permit (1/2 remaining). Unlike a mutex, any thread can release a semaphore permit.'
acceptanceCriteria: Mutex acquire: mentions OWNERSHIP explicitly
Mutex blocked: explains only one holder allowed, others must wait
Mutex release: mentions that ONLY the owner can release
Semaphore acquire: shows permit count and explains counting mechanism
RW Lock: explains multiple readers allowed, exclusive writer required
RW Lock writer priority: explains why writers block new readers

title: Add WHY descriptions to memory-alloc.ts — strategy selection explanations
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-077. Rewrite step descriptions for First/Best/Worst Fit. First Fit allocate: 'First Fit chose block at offset 40 (size 60B) — it scans from the beginning and selects the FIRST block large enough. Fast but can fragment the start of memory.' Best Fit: 'Best Fit chose the 45B block for a 40B request — it finds the TIGHTEST fit to minimize wasted space, but this can create tiny unusable fragments.'
acceptanceCriteria: First Fit: explains scan-from-start strategy and speed tradeoff
Best Fit: explains tightest-fit strategy and tiny-fragment risk
Worst Fit: explains largest-block strategy and leftover-space benefit
Failed allocation: explains why no block was large enough (fragmentation)
Deallocation: explains adjacent-block merging (coalescing)

title: Add prediction mode for CPU scheduling — 'Which process runs next?'
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-118 for CPU scheduling. Before each scheduling step, show ready queue and ask: 'Which process will the scheduler pick? A) P1 (burst=5, arrived t=0) B) P2 (burst=3, arrived t=1) C) P3 (burst=1, arrived t=2)'. Answer depends on active algorithm.
acceptanceCriteria: Prediction overlay appears before each 'start' or 'resume' event
Options show process details relevant to algorithm (burst for SJF, arrival for FCFS, priority for Priority)
Correct answer determined by the actual algorithm's next event
Feedback: 'Correct! SJF picks P3 because it has the shortest burst (1).'
Score tracker for this simulation run

title: Add prediction mode for page replacement — 'HIT or FAULT? Which evicted?'
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-118 for page replacement. Before each step, show current frames and next page. Ask: 'Will page 3 be a HIT or FAULT?' If FAULT with full frames: 'Which page will be evicted? A) Page 7 B) Page 0 C) Page 1'.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-phase prediction: first HIT/FAULT, then eviction choice if FAULT
Current frame contents clearly visible during prediction
For Optimal: future reference string visible so student can reason about it
Feedback explains the algorithm's eviction logic
Works for all 4 algorithms

title: Add prediction mode for virtual memory — 'TLB hit, PT hit, or page fault?'
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-118 for memory management. Before each translation step, show VA and ask: 'What will happen? A) TLB Hit B) TLB Miss + Page Table Hit C) Page Fault'. Student must reason about whether the page is in TLB, in page table, or neither.
acceptanceCriteria: 3-option prediction: TLB Hit / PT Hit / Page Fault
TLB contents visible during prediction (so student can check)
Feedback: 'Correct! Page 2 is in the TLB from the previous access — TLB Hit.'
Bonus follow-up on fault: 'Which frame will be evicted for this new page?'

title: Add prediction mode for thread sync — 'Will this thread acquire or block?'
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-118 for thread sync. Before each step, show current holder/waiters and ask: 'Thread T2 tries to acquire the mutex. Will it: A) Acquire (lock is free) B) Block (mutex held by T0)'. For semaphore: 'Permits: 0/2. T3 tries to acquire. What happens?'
acceptanceCriteria: 2-option prediction: Acquire / Block
Current mutex holder or semaphore permit count visible
For RW Lock: 'Will T3 (reader) be admitted while W0 (writer) holds the lock?'
Feedback explains the synchronization rule that determined the outcome

title: Replace hardcoded hex colors in SVG visualizations (RAG + ThreadSync)
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-024. The SVG components at OSModule.tsx:996-1094 (RAG) and 1177-1330 (ThreadSync) have ~25 hardcoded hex fills/strokes. Replace with CSS variables: #ef4444→var(--state-error), #22c55e→var(--state-success), #f59e0b→var(--state-warning), #6b7280→var(--foreground-muted), #06b6d4→var(--accent-cyan), #3b82f6→var(--primary).
acceptanceCriteria: All SVG fill= and stroke= attributes use CSS variables
RAG: assignment arrows use var(--state-success), request arrows use var(--foreground-muted), cycle arrows use var(--state-error)
ThreadSync: holding=var(--state-success), waiting=var(--state-warning), idle=var(--foreground-muted)
Colors adapt correctly in dark theme
No visual regression

title: Consolidate ALLOC_COLORS, THREAD_COLORS, PROCESS_COLORS into shared OS_PALETTE
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-024. Three arrays at OSModule.tsx:136-165 contain overlapping hex values. Create a single OS_PALETTE constant and derive the three named variants from it. Move to a shared file (e.g., src/lib/os/palette.ts).
acceptanceCriteria: Single OS_PALETTE array in src/lib/os/palette.ts
PROCESS_COLORS = OS_PALETTE.slice(0, 8)
THREAD_COLORS = OS_PALETTE.slice(0, 10)
ALLOC_COLORS = Object.fromEntries(OS_PALETTE.slice(0, 8).map((c, i) => [`P${i+1}`, c]))
No color duplication remains

title: Add mobile touch targets to CPU scheduling + page replacement panels
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-033. Focus on the two most-used concept panels. CPU scheduling: Run/Compare/Add Process buttons → min h-11 (44px). Process card inputs → stack vertically on mobile, min h-9. Trash icons → wrap with p-2 -m-2 for 44px tap zone. Page replacement: same button treatment + ref string input full-width.
acceptanceCriteria: All buttons in CPU scheduling and page replacement panels meet 44px minimum height
Process card inputs stack vertically below sm breakpoint
Trash/delete icons have invisible 44px tap zone (p-2 -m-2)
No overlapping tap targets on 375px viewport

title: Add mobile panel collapse — sidebar drawer + properties sheet
description: Atomic decomposition of OSC-033. Below md breakpoint: sidebar collapses behind hamburger button (use existing drawer pattern). Properties panel converts to bottom sheet (use existing PropertiesSheet at src/components/mobile/PropertiesSheet.tsx). Canvas fills full width.
acceptanceCriteria: Below md: sidebar hidden, hamburger icon in top bar opens drawer
Below md: properties panel replaced by bottom sheet trigger button
Canvas takes full width on mobile
Bottom panel respects safe-area-inset-bottom
Concept switching works from drawer
